Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Rapsodo MLM golf launch monitor and its smartphone app:

## Rapsodo MLM (Mobile Launch Monitor)

The Rapsodo MLM can make any iPhone or iPad a golf launch monitor for both indoor and outdoor use.(no content)It has been recognized as Golf Digest Editor's Choice and MyGolfSpy's Most Wanted launch monitor.

### Key Features

The Rapsodo Mobile Launch Monitor (MLM) pairs doppler-radar technology with your iPhone or iPad camera to provide instant video replay, active shot tracer, and professional-level accuracy (within 2% of Trackman) on: Distance, Club Speed, Ball Speed, Launch Angle, Launch Direction, Smash Factor and more.(no content)The device provides immediate swing replay with shot trace technology that turns your iPhone and iPad into a golf swing camera, along with 11 core metrics for total game analysis including carry distance, total distance, side carry, club speed, ball speed, launch angle, launch direction, and smash factor.

### Smartphone Connectivity

The Rapsodo MLM is a mobile, easy-to-use device that pairs to your smartphone showing a real-time breakdown of your swing.(no content)The MLM is automatically calibrated so you can simply download the app, connect your MLM via Bluetooth and start hitting.(no content)The MLM Golf Launch Monitor connects effortlessly to your mobile device via wireless Bluetooth.

### MLM2PRO (Newer Model)

The MLM2PRO platform is separate from the MLM platform and requires a separate app download. You can download the new MLM2PRO App from the Apple Store and Google Play.(no content)MLM2PRO is a launch monitor and golf simulator designed for complete visibility into your game. It's stacked with two advanced cameras plus Doppler Radar to provide video feedback and precise measurement on all golf shots. You can pair MLM2PRO with any Android, Apple phone, or tablet to record multiple angles of your swing.

### Setup Requirements

For outdoor setup, you should set the MLM 6-8ft behind you and the ball facing down your target line, with at least 60-80 ft. of ball flight.(no content)For net setup (indoor or outdoor), set the MLM 6-8ft behind you and the ball facing down your target line, with the net at least 8ft in front of the ball. The MLM needs at least 8ft of ball flight to capture data.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about PING nFlight golf club fitting app and its analytics:

## PING nFlight Fitting Technology

PING nFlight Web is a static fitting application - PING's online static fitting tool.

### nFlight Motion
PING announced nFlight Motion as an easy-to-use, highly-accurate fitting tool which recommends Ping driver-fitting specifications for golfers after only three shots.(no content)The SkyPro device attaches to the shaft directly below the grip and records a range of swing variables, including clubhead speed, transition, and shaft lean at impact, which are analysed through the nFlight Motion software to make loft, shaft flex and shaft model recommendations.

### Analytics Capabilities
Beavercreek Golf Club uses PING nFlight Fitting Technology as an industry-first virtual fitting software. By combining years of product knowledge, scientific data and fitting research, PING engineers developed this tool that integrates with golf ball launch monitors to precisely model, digitally analyze and scientifically compare every aspect of every shot it captures.(no content)This device, along with the nFlight Mobile App and an iPhone, enables fitters to recommend a Ping driver model, loft, and shaft after taking only 3 shots. The device provides an extremely accurate fitting, produces a 3D analysis of the player's swing, and has the ability to email results directly to the customer.(no content)According to John A. Solheim, PING chairman and CEO, "The ability to send swing data wirelessly to a mobile device represents an exciting evolution of our nFlight Fitting Software and nFlight Mobile technology." nFlight Motion's 3D swing playback is viewable from multiple angles.

### PING WebFit
PING WebFit is an online fitting questionnaire that allows you to get a better grasp on what PING clubs you should be fitted into.(no content)According to PING, "A static fitting through WEBFIT is a valuable starting point for educating you about important parameters of custom fitting," with 75% of players getting within one color code of their optimal when correctly filling out the form.

### Additional Tools
PING Co-Pilot is a golf fitting toolset developed by PING to give fitters access to the latest fitting technology.

input.content:
# Golf Platform Competitive Landscape Research

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Shot Tracking & Analytics Apps](#1-shot-tracking--analytics-apps)
2. [GPS & Rangefinder Apps](#2-gps--rangefinder-apps)
3. [Handicap & Score Tracking](#3-handicap--score-tracking)
4. [League Management Software](#4-league-management-software)
5. [Tournament Management Software](#5-tournament-management-software)
6. [Social & Community Platforms](#6-social--community-platforms)
7. [Betting & Side Games Apps](#7-betting--side-games-apps)
8. [Launch Monitors & Hardware](#8-launch-monitors--hardware)
9. [Tee Time Booking Platforms](#9-tee-time-booking-platforms)
10. [Amateur Golf Organizations](#10-amateur-golf-organizations)
11. [Club Fitting Technology](#11-club-fitting-technology)

---

## 1. Shot Tracking & Analytics Apps

### Arccos Golf
- **Website:** [arccosgolf.com](https://www.arccosgolf.com)
- **Type:** Hardware sensors + App (iOS/Android)
- **Pricing:** ~$179 sensors + ~$99/year subscription
- **Key Features:**
  - AI-powered Caddie with personalized club recommendations
  - Strokes Gained analytics (considered best-in-class)
  - Auto shot tracking via grip sensors
  - 40,000+ course database
  - Partnership with Cobra (pre-installed in grips)
  - Microsoft partnership for AI development
- **Differentiator:** Best analytics dashboard, Tour-level strokes gained data
- **Sources:** [Golf Monthly](https://www.golfmonthly.com/best-golf-deals/best-golf-shot-tracking-apps-and-devices-213502), [MyGolfSpy Forum](https://forum.mygolfspy.com/topic/59818-shot-tracking-options-arccos-garmin-shotscope-apple-watch/)

### Shot Scope
- **Website:** [shotscope.com](https://www.shotscope.com)
- **Type:** GPS Watch + Sensors
- **Pricing:** $219.99 one-time (no subscription)
- **Key Features:**
  - V5 GPS Watch with shot tracking
  - "Pin Collect" feature for exact pin locations
  - Auto club detection
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - No ongoing subscription costs
- **Differentiator:** Best value (no subscription), excellent wearable
- **Sources:** [Today's Golfer](https://www.todays-golfer.com/equipment/best/golf-gps-shot-tracking-devices-us/)

### Clippd
- **Website:** [clippd.com](https://www.clippd.com)
- **Type:** Analytics aggregator app
- **Pricing:** $25/month or $239.99/year
- **Key Features:**
  - AI-powered insights
  - Shot Quality and Player Quality metrics (0-200 scale)
  - "What To Work On" personalized priority list
  - Integrates with Arccos, Garmin, TrackMan
  - Used by top college golf programs
  - Factors in weather and course conditions
- **Differentiator:** Best for serious improvement, aggregates data from multiple sources
- **Sources:** [Golf Monthly Review](https://www.golfmonthly.com/reviews/golf-tech-and-training-aids/clippd-review), [Clippd](https://www.clippd.com)

### V1 Golf (Swing Analysis)
- **Website:** [v1sports.com](https://v1sports.com/athletes/v1-golf-app/)
- **Type:** Video swing analysis app
- **Pricing:** Free basic, $59.99/year or $6.99/month premium
- **Key Features:**
  - HD video capture and analysis
  - Side-by-side comparison with pros
  - Drawing tools for swing planes
  - Cloud storage for all sessions
  - Remote lessons from coaches
- **Differentiator:** Best video swing analysis tool
- **Sources:** [V1 Sports](https://v1sports.com/athletes/v1-golf-app/), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/v1-golf-golf-swing-analyzer/id349715369)

### V1 Game (Stats/Strokes Gained)
- **Website:** [v1sports.com](https://v1sports.com)
- **Type:** Stats and strokes gained companion app
- **Pricing:** $59.99/year or $6.99/month after 3 free rounds
- **Key Features:**
  - Strokes gained by category (driving, approach, short game, putting)
  - Shot patterns and club distances
  - Traditional stats (FIR, GIR, putts)
  - 9-tab post-round analysis
- **Sources:** [AmateurGolf.com](https://www.amateurgolf.com/golf-tournament-news/28581/V1-Game--The-best-game-tracking-and-strokes-gained-app)

### Golf Pad GPS
- **Website:** [golfpadgps.com](https://golfpadgps.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Shot tracking app
- **Pricing:** $99 TAGS + $29.99/year premium
- **Key Features:**
  - Shot tracking with TAGS sensors
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - Apple Watch integration
  - "Plays Like" distances
  - Data export to Excel
- **Differentiator:** Most affordable shot tracking, excellent Apple Watch support
- **Sources:** [Golf Pad](https://golfpadgps.com), [GolfPass Review](https://www.golfpass.com/travel-advisor/articles/golfpad-club-sensor-shot-tracking-app)

---

## 2. GPS & Rangefinder Apps

### 18Birdies
- **Website:** [18birdies.com](https://18birdies.com)
- **Type:** GPS, Scoring, Social app
- **Pricing:** Free basic, $99.99/year premium (Caddy+)
- **Key Features:**
  - 43,000+ courses
  - GPS rangefinder with "Plays Like" distances
  - AI Swing Analyzer
  - Side games (Skins, Wolf, Nassau, etc.)
  - Tournaments with live leaderboards
  - 3D Green Maps (premium)
  - Apple Watch/Wear OS support
- **Stats:** 2.2M+ downloads, 4.85/5 rating
- **Differentiator:** Most complete free features, strong social/betting games
- **Sources:** [18Birdies](https://18birdies.com/features/), [Golf Monthly](https://www.golfmonthly.com/reviews/gps/18birdies-app-review-is-this-the-best-free-golf-gps-app)

### Golfshot
- **Website:** [golfshot.com](https://golfshot.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Analytics app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - 45,000+ courses worldwide
  - Auto Shot Tracking (Apple Watch)
  - Swing ID (tempo, hand speed, swing path)
  - Golfscape AR (augmented reality distances)
  - Green Maps
  - Practice range integration
  - Personalized club recommendations
- **Differentiator:** Best AR feature, practice range integration
- **Sources:** [Golfshot](https://golfshot.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golfshot-golf-gps-watch/id622851626)

### GolfLogix
- **Website:** [golflogix.com](https://www.golflogix.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Putt Reading app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - 3D putt line visualization
  - Green heat maps (slope and speed)
  - One-touch putt reading
  - GPS with slope adjustments
  - 8,000+ courses with putt data
  - Apple Watch support
  - VR hole previews and flyovers
- **Stats:** 5M+ users worldwide
- **Differentiator:** Best putt reading/green analysis
- **Sources:** [GolfLogix](https://www.golflogix.com), [PRNewswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/golfs-no-1-app-enhances-putt-breaks-with-3d-greens-maps-and-one-touch-putt-reading-300672674.html)

### Bushnell Golf App
- **Website:** [bushnellgolf.com](https://www.bushnellgolf.com)
- **Type:** GPS companion app (free with hardware)
- **Features:**
  - 38,000+ courses
  - Shot planning with movable layup points
  - Full-color GPS hole maps
  - Device firmware updates via Bluetooth
  - Swing Pro for swing analysis
  - Step tracking/fitness data
- **2025 Hardware:** Tour Hybrid (first laser + GPS combo)
- **Sources:** [Bushnell Golf](https://www.bushnellgolf.com), [Bunkered](https://www.bunkered.co.uk/gear/why-you-should-download-the-free-bushnell-gps-app/)

### Garmin Golf App
- **Website:** [garmin.com/golf](https://www.garmin.com/en-US/p/605172/)
- **Type:** GPS + Shot tracking (with CT10 sensors)
- **Pricing:** Free app, $100/year optional, CT10 sensors ~$299
- **Key Features:**
  - Pairs with Garmin watches and CT10 sensors
  - Auto shot tracking with full sensor set
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - Weekly leaderboards and private tournaments
  - Works with Approach R10 launch monitor
- **Limitation:** Requires Garmin watch, analytics less robust than Arccos
- **Sources:** [Garmin](https://www.garmin.com/en-US/p/605172/), [Breaking Eighty](https://breakingeighty.com/garmin-ct10-sensors-review)

---

## 3. Handicap & Score Tracking

### GHIN (USGA Official)
- **Website:** [ghin.com](https://www.ghin.com)

  - Score posting (total, hole-by-hole, with stats)
  - Course maps and GPS
  - Putt break maps (premium)
  - Apple Watch support
  - GHIN Challenges (virtual leaderboards)
- **Stats:** 3.2M+ US golfers
- **Differentiator:** Only official USGA handicap
- **Sources:** [GHIN](https://www.ghin.com), [USGA](https://www.usga.org/content/usga/home-page/articles/2021/10/new-features-ghin-mobile-app-give-golfers-helping-hand.html)

### SwingU

### Hole19
- **Website:** [hole19golf.com](https://www.hole19golf.com)

- **Pricing:** Free basic, premium subscription

## 4. League Management Software

## 7. Betting & Side Games Apps

### Beezer Golf

---

## 8. Launch Monitors & Hardware

### TrackMan
- **Website:** [trackman.com](https://www.trackman.com)
- **Type:** Premium launch monitor + simulator
- **Key Features:**
  - Indoor/outdoor use
  - AI-driven swing analysis
  - Automatic shot capture
  - Speed Training mode
  - Lifetime statistics and handicap
  - FSX Play simulation software
  - Multiple languages
- **Used by:** Tour professionals worldwide
- **Differentiator:** Gold standard for accuracy
- **Sources:** [TrackMan](https://www.trackman.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trackman-golf/id1245413916)

### Foresight Sports (GCQuad)
- **Website:** [foresightsports.com](https://www.foresightsports.com)
- **Type:** Premium launch monitor
- **Key Features:**
  - Quadrascopic imaging (most accurate)
  - Ball + club head data
  - FSX Pro (fitting/instruction)
  - FSX Play (Unity-powered simulation)
  - Putting analysis
  - Indoor/outdoor use
  - No calibration needed
- **Data:** Ball velocity, spin, launch angle, club speed, smash factor, impact location
- **Differentiator:** Most accurate, comprehensive data
- **Sources:** [Foresight Sports](https://www.foresightsports.com/pages/gcquad)

### Rapsodo MLM
- **Website:** [rapsodo.com](https://rapsodo.com)
- **Type:** Smartphone-based launch monitor
- **Pricing:** Affordable entry point
- **Key Features:**

  - Doppler radar + camera
  - Instant video replay with shot trace
  - Within 2% of TrackMan accuracy
  - 11 core metrics
  - Auto-calibration
  - Indoor/outdoor use
- **MLM2PRO:** Newer model with dual cameras
- **Differentiator:** Most affordable accurate launch monitor
- **Sources:** [Rapsodo](https://rapsodo.com/products/mobile-launch-monitor)

### Garmin Approach R10
- **Website:** [garmin.com](https://www.garmin.com)
- **Type:** Portable launch monitor
- **Features:**

  - Portable design
- **Sources:** [Garmin Forums](https://forums.garmin.com)

## 9. Tee Time Booking Platforms

### GolfNow

- **Type:** Tee time marketplace
- **Features:**

  - 24/7 instant booking
  - Hot Deals with weather protection
  - GPS and rangefinder
  - Scorekeeping
  - GolfNow Rewards
  - GolfPass integration

- **Differentiator:** Largest marketplace, best deals
- **Sources:** [GolfNow](https://www.golfnow.com/mobile)

### Supreme Golf

- **Type:** Tee time aggregator
- **Features:**

---

## 10. Amateur Golf Organizations

### USGA (United States Golf Association)
- **Website:** [usga.org](https://www.usga.org)
- **Type:** Governing body
- **Key Activities:**
  - Rules of Golf (with The R&A)
  - 14 national championships (10 amateur)
  - U.S. Open, U.S. Women's Open
  - GHIN handicap system
  - Curtis Cup, Walker Cup
- **Reach:** 25M+ golfers, 150+ golf associations
- **Sources:** [USGA](https://www.usga.org), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Golf_Association)

### Mediocre Golf Association
- See Social & Community section

---

## 11. Club Fitting Technology

### PING nFlight
- **Website:** [nflightweb.ping.com](https://nflightweb.ping.com)
- **Type:** Club fitting technology
- **Features:**
  - nFlight Web (static fitting)
  - nFlight Motion (3 shots = driver recommendation)
  - WebFit (online questionnaire)
  - Co-Pilot (fitter toolset)
  - 3D swing analysis
  - Integrates with launch monitors
- **Differentiator:** Industry-first virtual fitting software
- **Sources:** [PING nFlight](https://nflightweb.ping.com), [PING WebFit](https://webfit.ping.com)

### Cobra Connect
- **Website:** [cobragolf.com/pages/arccos](https://www.cobragolf.com/pages/arccos)
- **Type:** Pre-installed Arccos sensors
- **Features:**
  - Sensors embedded in Golf Pride grips
  - Full Arccos Caddie integration
  - Free sensors + 45-day trial with club purchase
  - Auto shot tracking
  - Strokes gained
- **Differentiator:** Only OEM with built-in shot tracking
- **Sources:** [Cobra Golf](https://www.cobragolf.com/pages/arccos), [Arccos Golf](https://www.arccosgolf.com/pages/cobra)

---

## Comparison: Sports Team Management (Non-Golf)

These platforms inspired Lazy Golfing's feature set:

### Project Hockey
- **Website:** [project.hockey](https://project.hockey)
- **Type:** Hockey league management
- **Features:**
  - Team rosters
  - Scheduling
  - Stats tracking
  - Standings
  - Communication

### TeamSnap
- **Website:** [teamsnap.com](https://www.teamsnap.com)
- **Type:** Multi-sport team management
- **Features:**
  - Scheduling
  - Availability tracking
  - Messaging
  - Payments
  - Stats
  - Photo sharing

---

## Market Gaps & Opportunities

Based on this research, Lazy Golfing can differentiate by:

1. **Unified Platform:** Most apps specialize (GPS OR betting OR leagues). Combine all.
2. **True League Management for Golf:** Golf Genius is enterprise-priced. Mid-market gap exists.
3. **Social + Competition:** MGA culture without chapter structure/geography limits.
4. **Free Tier:** Compete with TheGrint/18Birdies free features.
5. **Playing Partner Matching:** Deemples only works in Asia. US gap.
6. **No Hardware Lock-in:** Unlike Arccos/Garmin, work without sensors.
7. **Cart Golf Focus:** Unique positioning vs. serious golfer apps.

---

*This document should be updated as new competitors emerge or existing ones pivot.*

# Golf Platform Competitive Landscape Research

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Shot Tracking & Analytics Apps](#1-shot-tracking--analytics-apps)
2. [GPS & Rangefinder Apps](#2-gps--rangefinder-apps)
3. [Handicap & Score Tracking](#3-handicap--score-tracking)
4. [League Management Software](#4-league-management-software)
5. [Tournament Management Software](#5-tournament-management-software)
6. [Social & Community Platforms](#6-social--community-platforms)
7. [Betting & Side Games Apps](#7-betting--side-games-apps)
8. [Launch Monitors & Hardware](#8-launch-monitors--hardware)
9. [Tee Time Booking Platforms](#9-tee-time-booking-platforms)
10. [Amateur Golf Organizations](#10-amateur-golf-organizations)
11. [Club Fitting Technology](#11-club-fitting-technology)

---

## 1. Shot Tracking & Analytics Apps

### Arccos Golf
- **Website:** [arccosgolf.com](https://www.arccosgolf.com)
- **Type:** Hardware sensors + App (iOS/Android)
- **Pricing:** ~$179 sensors + ~$99/year subscription
- **Key Features:**
  - AI-powered Caddie with personalized club recommendations
  - Strokes Gained analytics (considered best-in-class)
  - Auto shot tracking via grip sensors
  - 40,000+ course database
  - Partnership with Cobra (pre-installed in grips)
  - Microsoft partnership for AI development
- **Differentiator:** Best analytics dashboard, Tour-level strokes gained data
- **Sources:** [Golf Monthly](https://www.golfmonthly.com/best-golf-deals/best-golf-shot-tracking-apps-and-devices-213502), [MyGolfSpy Forum](https://forum.mygolfspy.com/topic/59818-shot-tracking-options-arccos-garmin-shotscope-apple-watch/)

### Shot Scope
- **Website:** [shotscope.com](https://www.shotscope.com)
- **Type:** GPS Watch + Sensors
- **Pricing:** $219.99 one-time (no subscription)
- **Key Features:**
  - V5 GPS Watch with shot tracking
  - "Pin Collect" feature for exact pin locations
  - Auto club detection
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - No ongoing subscription costs
- **Differentiator:** Best value (no subscription), excellent wearable
- **Sources:** [Today's Golfer](https://www.todays-golfer.com/equipment/best/golf-gps-shot-tracking-devices-us/)

### Clippd
- **Website:** [clippd.com](https://www.clippd.com)
- **Type:** Analytics aggregator app
- **Pricing:** $25/month or $239.99/year
- **Key Features:**
  - AI-powered insights
  - Shot Quality and Player Quality metrics (0-200 scale)
  - "What To Work On" personalized priority list
  - Integrates with Arccos, Garmin, TrackMan
  - Used by top college golf programs
  - Factors in weather and course conditions
- **Differentiator:** Best for serious improvement, aggregates data from multiple sources
- **Sources:** [Golf Monthly Review](https://www.golfmonthly.com/reviews/golf-tech-and-training-aids/clippd-review), [Clippd](https://www.clippd.com)

### V1 Golf (Swing Analysis)
- **Website:** [v1sports.com](https://v1sports.com/athletes/v1-golf-app/)
- **Type:** Video swing analysis app
- **Pricing:** Free basic, $59.99/year or $6.99/month premium
- **Key Features:**
  - HD video capture and analysis
  - Side-by-side comparison with pros
  - Drawing tools for swing planes
  - Cloud storage for all sessions
  - Remote lessons from coaches
- **Differentiator:** Best video swing analysis tool
- **Sources:** [V1 Sports](https://v1sports.com/athletes/v1-golf-app/), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/v1-golf-golf-swing-analyzer/id349715369)

### V1 Game (Stats/Strokes Gained)
- **Website:** [v1sports.com](https://v1sports.com)
- **Type:** Stats and strokes gained companion app
- **Pricing:** $59.99/year or $6.99/month after 3 free rounds
- **Key Features:**
  - Strokes gained by category (driving, approach, short game, putting)
  - Shot patterns and club distances
  - Traditional stats (FIR, GIR, putts)
  - 9-tab post-round analysis
- **Sources:** [AmateurGolf.com](https://www.amateurgolf.com/golf-tournament-news/28581/V1-Game--The-best-game-tracking-and-strokes-gained-app)

### Golf Pad GPS
- **Website:** [golfpadgps.com](https://golfpadgps.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Shot tracking app
- **Pricing:** $99 TAGS + $29.99/year premium
- **Key Features:**
  - Shot tracking with TAGS sensors
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - Apple Watch integration
  - "Plays Like" distances
  - Data export to Excel
- **Differentiator:** Most affordable shot tracking, excellent Apple Watch support
- **Sources:** [Golf Pad](https://golfpadgps.com), [GolfPass Review](https://www.golfpass.com/travel-advisor/articles/golfpad-club-sensor-shot-tracking-app)

---

## 2. GPS & Rangefinder Apps

### 18Birdies
- **Website:** [18birdies.com](https://18birdies.com)
- **Type:** GPS, Scoring, Social app
- **Pricing:** Free basic, $99.99/year premium (Caddy+)
- **Key Features:**
  - 43,000+ courses
  - GPS rangefinder with "Plays Like" distances
  - AI Swing Analyzer
  - Side games (Skins, Wolf, Nassau, etc.)
  - Tournaments with live leaderboards
  - 3D Green Maps (premium)
  - Apple Watch/Wear OS support
- **Stats:** 2.2M+ downloads, 4.85/5 rating
- **Differentiator:** Most complete free features, strong social/betting games
- **Sources:** [18Birdies](https://18birdies.com/features/), [Golf Monthly](https://www.golfmonthly.com/reviews/gps/18birdies-app-review-is-this-the-best-free-golf-gps-app)

### Golfshot
- **Website:** [golfshot.com](https://golfshot.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Analytics app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - 45,000+ courses worldwide
  - Auto Shot Tracking (Apple Watch)
  - Swing ID (tempo, hand speed, swing path)
  - Golfscape AR (augmented reality distances)
  - Green Maps
  - Practice range integration
  - Personalized club recommendations
- **Differentiator:** Best AR feature, practice range integration
- **Sources:** [Golfshot](https://golfshot.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golfshot-golf-gps-watch/id622851626)

### GolfLogix
- **Website:** [golflogix.com](https://www.golflogix.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Putt Reading app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - 3D putt line visualization
  - Green heat maps (slope and speed)
  - One-touch putt reading
  - GPS with slope adjustments
  - 8,000+ courses with putt data
  - Apple Watch support
  - VR hole previews and flyovers
- **Stats:** 5M+ users worldwide
- **Differentiator:** Best putt reading/green analysis
- **Sources:** [GolfLogix](https://www.golflogix.com), [PRNewswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/golfs-no-1-app-enhances-putt-breaks-with-3d-greens-maps-and-one-touch-putt-reading-300672674.html)

### Bushnell Golf App
- **Website:** [bushnellgolf.com](https://www.bushnellgolf.com)
- **Type:** GPS companion app (free with hardware)
- **Features:**
  - 38,000+ courses
  - Shot planning with movable layup points
  - Full-color GPS hole maps
  - Device firmware updates via Bluetooth
  - Swing Pro for swing analysis
  - Step tracking/fitness data
- **2025 Hardware:** Tour Hybrid (first laser + GPS combo)
- **Sources:** [Bushnell Golf](https://www.bushnellgolf.com), [Bunkered](https://www.bunkered.co.uk/gear/why-you-should-download-the-free-bushnell-gps-app/)

### Garmin Golf App
- **Website:** [garmin.com/golf](https://www.garmin.com/en-US/p/605172/)
- **Type:** GPS + Shot tracking (with CT10 sensors)
- **Pricing:** Free app, $100/year optional, CT10 sensors ~$299
- **Key Features:**
  - Pairs with Garmin watches and CT10 sensors
  - Auto shot tracking with full sensor set
  - Strokes gained analysis
  - Weekly leaderboards and private tournaments
  - Works with Approach R10 launch monitor
- **Limitation:** Requires Garmin watch, analytics less robust than Arccos
- **Sources:** [Garmin](https://www.garmin.com/en-US/p/605172/), [Breaking Eighty](https://breakingeighty.com/garmin-ct10-sensors-review)

---

## 3. Handicap & Score Tracking

### GHIN (USGA Official)
- **Website:** [ghin.com](https://www.ghin.com)
- **Type:** Official USGA handicap system
- **Pricing:** Free trial (54 holes), $49.99/year enhanced GPS
- **Key Features:**
  - Official Handicap Index calculation
  - World Handicap System compliant
  - Score posting (total, hole-by-hole, with stats)
  - Course maps and GPS
  - Putt break maps (premium)
  - Apple Watch support
  - GHIN Challenges (virtual leaderboards)
- **Stats:** 3.2M+ US golfers
- **Differentiator:** Only official USGA handicap
- **Sources:** [GHIN](https://www.ghin.com), [USGA](https://www.usga.org/content/usga/home-page/articles/2021/10/new-features-ghin-mobile-app-give-golfers-helping-hand.html)

### TheGrint
- **Website:** [thegrint.com](https://thegrint.com)
- **Type:** Handicap + Social app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - Licensed USGA handicap data affiliate
  - Large golfer community
  - Leaderboards and competitions
  - Multiple game formats (Medal, Stableford, Skins, Vegas, Wolf)
  - Photo sharing
  - 40,000+ courses
- **Differentiator:** Official USGA partnership, strong community features
- **Sources:** [TheGrint](https://thegrint.com), [Golf Insider UK](https://golfinsideruk.com/best-golf-handicap-app/)

### SwingU
- **Website:** [swingu.com](https://swingu.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Handicap + Coaching app
- **Pricing:** Free handicap, premium subscription
- **Key Features:**
  - Free handicap posting
  - GPS rangefinder
  - Strokes gained (premium)
  - Tips and drills based on your data
  - Green maps
  - Smartwatch support
- **Differentiator:** Best for beginners (includes coaching content)
- **Sources:** [SwingU Clubhouse](https://clubhouse.swingu.com/lifestyle/the-ultimate-guide-to-the-best-golf-apps-in-2024/)

### Hole19
- **Website:** [hole19golf.com](https://www.hole19golf.com)
- **Type:** GPS + Social app
- **Pricing:** Free basic, premium subscription
- **Key Features:**
  - 43,000+ courses
  - Stat tracking
  - Strong social features (share rounds, compete)
  - Smartwatch integration
  - **Offline mode** (vital for remote courses)
  - Club recommendations (premium)
- **Stats:** 1M+ users
- **Differentiator:** Best offline support, strong social
- **Sources:** [Green Golfing Genius](https://greengolfinggenius.com/top-golf-apps-to-track-your-scores-my-top-picks/)

---

## 4. League Management Software

### Golf Genius
- **Website:** [golfgenius.com](https://golfgenius.com)
- **Type:** Enterprise tournament/league management
- **Pricing:** $200 onboarding (basic), $500 (premium), multi-product discounts up to $2,000
- **Key Features:**
  - Industry-leading platform for clubs/associations
  - Powers 40M+ annual rounds
  - Live scoring and leaderboards
  - Online registration and payments
  - Custom event portals
  - Mobile app (GGS)
  - USGA partnership (free for GHIN clubs as TM Club)
- **Clients:** USGA, European Tour, PGAs worldwide
- **Stats:** 11,000+ facilities in 60+ countries
- **Differentiator:** Most comprehensive, used by major organizations
- **Sources:** [Golf Genius](https://golfgenius.com), [FSGA](https://www.fsga.org/sections/Clubs/About-Golf-Genius/868)

### League Golfer
- **Website:** [leaguegolfer.com](https://www.leaguegolfer.com)
- **Type:** League management software
- **Features:**
  - Automatic scheduling
  - Live scoring and leaderboard
  - Handicap calculation
  - Mobile app with push notifications
  - Instant messaging
- **Sources:** [League Golfer](https://www.leaguegolfer.com)

### x4Golf
- **Website:** [x4golf.com](https://www.x4golf.com)
- **Type:** League management software
- **Pricing:** $55/year (unlimited players, courses, rounds)
- **Features:**
  - Live scoring with leaderboard
  - Customizable handicaps
  - Scheduling
  - Score tracking
- **Differentiator:** Most affordable option
- **Sources:** [x4Golf](https://www.x4golf.com)

### Golf League Manager (GolfSoftware.com)
- **Website:** [golfsoftware.com](https://golfsoftware.com)
- **Type:** Desktop + Web software
- **Features:**
  - 30+ years in business
  - Auto schedule building
  - 9 or 18 hole leagues
  - Individual or team leagues
  - Multiple divisions/flights
  - Handicap/quota/points updates
- **Sources:** [GolfSoftware](https://golfsoftware.com/lm/features.html)

### LeagueSync
- **Website:** [golf.leaguesync.com](http://golf.leaguesync.com)
- **Type:** Free league management
- **Features:**
  - Free scoring and handicap
  - Team scoring
  - Scheduling and tee times
  - Player statistics
  - Reports
- **Differentiator:** Completely free
- **Sources:** [LeagueSync](http://golf.leaguesync.com)

### netGolfLeague
- **Website:** [netgolfleague.com](https://www.netgolfleague.com)
- **Type:** Online league software
- **Features:**
  - Automatic match scheduling
  - Weekly pairings and tee times
  - 1,000+ leagues worldwide
- **Sources:** [netGolfLeague](https://www.netgolfleague.com)

---

## 5. Tournament Management Software

### Golf Genius Tournament Management
- **Website:** [golfgenius.com/products/tm](https://golfgenius.com/products/tm)
- **Pricing:** See League Management section
- **Key Features:**
  - All tournament formats
  - Live scoring
  - Online registration/payments
  - Custom event websites
  - GGS mobile app
- **Sources:** See League Management section

### GolfStatus
- **Website:** [golfstatus.com](https://golfstatus.com)
- **Type:** Tournament management for nonprofits
- **Pricing:** Free through Golf for Good program
- **Key Features:**
  - Online registration
  - Live scoring/leaderboards
  - Digital sponsorships
  - Fundraising tools
  - Payment processing
  - Collaboration tools
- **Differentiator:** Best for charity/nonprofit events
- **Sources:** [GolfStatus](https://golfstatus.com), [Software Advice](https://www.softwareadvice.com/nonprofit/golfstatus-profile/)

### DoJiggy
- **Website:** [dojiggy.com/golf-tournament-software](https://www.dojiggy.com/golf-tournament-software/)
- **Type:** Free tournament platform
- **Pricing:** Free full-featured solution
- **Key Features:**
  - Online registration
  - Sponsorship management
  - Text-to-donate
  - Online auctions
  - Live streaming
  - Fundraising thermometer
  - Team pairings
- **Differentiator:** Best free option with full features
- **Sources:** [DoJiggy](https://www.dojiggy.com/golf-tournament-software/)

### BlueGolf
- **Website:** [bluegolf.com](https://www.bluegolf.com)
- **Type:** Tournament management software
- **Pricing:** $199/year (basic) to $999/year (premium)
- **Key Features:**
  - All tournament formats
  - Quick pairings generation
  - Live scoring via app
  - Real-time leaderboards
  - Professional, amateur, and junior support
- **Clients:** Clubessential Holdings group
- **Sources:** [BlueGolf](https://www.bluegolf.com), [Golf Course Technology Reviews](https://www.golfcoursetechnologyreviews.org/golf-course-vendors/bluegolf)

### Golf GameBook
- **Website:** [golfgamebook.com](https://www.golfgamebook.com)
- **Type:** Scorecard + Tournament app
- **Pricing:** Freemium model
- **Key Features:**
  - 20+ game formats
  - Live leaderboards
  - Up to 72 players per tournament
  - Ryder Cup-style team competitions
  - Tournament Manager web service
  - Large-screen leaderboard display
  - 42,000+ courses GPS
- **Stats:** 1.5M+ golfers, 35M+ rounds
- **Differentiator:** Best for large group/team events
- **Sources:** [Golf GameBook](https://www.golfgamebook.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golf-gamebook-scorecard-gps/id409307935)

---

## 6. Social & Community Platforms

### Mediocre Golf Association (MGA)
- **Website:** [mgatour.com](https://mgatour.com)
- **Type:** Recreational golf league organization
- **Pricing:** Chapter-based membership
- **Key Features:**
  - 8 stroke play tournaments per year
  - Trophies, awards, "kinda big checks"
  - Money lists and stats for all players
  - MGA World Championship in Vegas
  - Chapters worldwide
  - Self-deprecating culture ("Face it, you suck")
- **Founded:** 2006 in San Francisco
- **Tagline:** "The official golf association for the other guys"
- **Differentiator:** Fun, inclusive community for average golfers
- **Sources:** [MGA Tour](https://mgatour.com), [MGAWC](https://mgawc.com)

### GolfLync
- **Website:** [golflync.com](https://www.sportlync.com)
- **Type:** Golf social network
- **Features:**
  - 100,000+ golfers
  - Virtual Golf Clubs (VGC)
  - Group chats
  - Player matching
  - Tee time scheduling
  - Community events
- **Sources:** [GolfLync App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golflync-golfing-social-media/id1618788903)

### Deemples
- **Website:** [deemples.com](https://deemples.com)
- **Type:** Golf buddy finder + Booking
- **Availability:** Southeast Asia primarily (Indonesia, Philippines, Singapore, Malaysia, Brunei, Australia)
- **Features:**
  - Find playing partners
  - Book tee times
  - Online payments
  - USGA handicap tracking
  - Chat with playing partners
- **Limitation:** Limited to Southeast Asia/Australia
- **Sources:** [Deemples](https://deemples.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/deemples-find-golf-buddies/id1141723947)

### Golf Lounge
- **Website:** [golflounge.app](https://www.golflounge.app)
- **Type:** Golf social network
- **Features:**
  - Focused on before/after round connections
  - Building relationships
  - Sharing experiences
  - Inclusive community
- **Differentiator:** Relationship-focused vs. on-course features
- **Sources:** [Golf Lounge](https://www.golflounge.app)

### 19thole
- **Website:** (Various app stores)
- **Type:** Golf social network
- **Features:**
  - Social connections
  - Game arrangement
  - Networking
  - Backed by Ian Woosnam
- **Sources:** [Golf Monthly](https://www.golfmonthly.com/news/19thole-app-launched-new-social-network-for-golfers-247402)

### Swing Social
- **Website:** [swingsocial.gaxa.golf](https://swingsocial.gaxa.golf)
- **Type:** Social golf club platform
- **Features:**
  - Plan group outings
  - Organize tournaments
  - Find co-players
  - Game-by-game competition
- **Sources:** [Swing Social](https://swingsocial.gaxa.golf)

### Fairway Friends Golf
- **Type:** Golf buddy matching app
- **Features:**
  - Match by handicap and preferences
  - Browse local players
  - Send invites
  - Chat to plan rounds
- **Sources:** [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/fairway-friends-golf/id6749872932)

---

## 7. Betting & Side Games Apps

### Beezer Golf
- **Website:** [beezergolf.com](https://beezergolf.com)
- **Type:** Side games scorecard app
- **Features:**
  - ~30 games with multiple configurations
  - Up to 120 players
  - BEEZER Bank for bet tracking across rounds
  - Efficient payout calculation
  - GPS rangefinder included
- **Differentiator:** Largest game library, ongoing balance tracking
- **Sources:** [Beezer Golf](https://beezergolf.com)

### Skins App
- **Website:** (App Store)
- **Pricing:** $40/year
- **Features:**
  - 13 game formats
  - Stroke Play, Match Play, Nassau
  - Nine Point, Banker, Wolf
  - Trophies and payouts tracking
- **Sources:** [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/skins-app/id6447497626)

### Unknown Golf
- **Website:** [unknowngolf.com](https://www.unknowngolf.com)
- **Type:** Wagering app
- **Features:**
  - Skins, Dot/Trash, Banker, Vegas
  - Live tracking
  - Integrated Venmo settlement
- **Sources:** [Unknown Golf](https://www.unknowngolf.com/golf-wager-app)

### Golf Bettor
- **Website:** (App Store)
- **Features:**
  - 12 game formats, 65 options
  - 18,000 courses (US/Canada)
  - Auto-Press and Optional Press
  - Mid-round bet additions
- **Sources:** [PRNewswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/golf-bettor-the-most-complete-betting-mobile-app-for-avid-golfers-launches-on-apple-play-store-301045048.html)

### GolfApp
- **Website:** [golfapp.com](https://golfapp.com)
- **Type:** Scoring and betting app
- **Features:**
  - Nassau, Skins, Match Play
  - Nine Point, Closeout, Wolf
  - Regular game additions
- **Sources:** [GolfApp](https://golfapp.com)

### Loop Golf
- **Website:** (App stores)
- **Features:**
  - Tee time booking
  - GPS tracking
  - Scoring
  - Integrated wager + payment under one umbrella
- **Differentiator:** Combined betting + payment in one app
- **Sources:** [Golf.com](https://golf.com/lifestyle/loopgolf-computing-paying-golf-bets/)

---

## 8. Launch Monitors & Hardware

### TrackMan
- **Website:** [trackman.com](https://www.trackman.com)
- **Type:** Premium launch monitor + simulator
- **Key Features:**
  - Indoor/outdoor use
  - AI-driven swing analysis
  - Automatic shot capture
  - Speed Training mode
  - Lifetime statistics and handicap
  - FSX Play simulation software
  - Multiple languages
- **Used by:** Tour professionals worldwide
- **Differentiator:** Gold standard for accuracy
- **Sources:** [TrackMan](https://www.trackman.com), [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trackman-golf/id1245413916)

### Foresight Sports (GCQuad)
- **Website:** [foresightsports.com](https://www.foresightsports.com)
- **Type:** Premium launch monitor
- **Key Features:**
  - Quadrascopic imaging (most accurate)
  - Ball + club head data
  - FSX Pro (fitting/instruction)
  - FSX Play (Unity-powered simulation)
  - Putting analysis
  - Indoor/outdoor use
  - No calibration needed
- **Data:** Ball velocity, spin, launch angle, club speed, smash factor, impact location
- **Differentiator:** Most accurate, comprehensive data
- **Sources:** [Foresight Sports](https://www.foresightsports.com/pages/gcquad)

### Rapsodo MLM
- **Website:** [rapsodo.com](https://rapsodo.com)
- **Type:** Smartphone-based launch monitor
- **Pricing:** Affordable entry point
- **Key Features:**
  - Pairs with iPhone/iPad
  - Doppler radar + camera
  - Instant video replay with shot trace
  - Within 2% of TrackMan accuracy
  - 11 core metrics
  - Auto-calibration
  - Indoor/outdoor use
- **MLM2PRO:** Newer model with dual cameras
- **Differentiator:** Most affordable accurate launch monitor
- **Sources:** [Rapsodo](https://rapsodo.com/products/mobile-launch-monitor)

### Garmin Approach R10
- **Website:** [garmin.com](https://www.garmin.com)
- **Type:** Portable launch monitor
- **Features:**
  - Integrates with Garmin Golf app
  - Simulation capabilities
  - Portable design
- **Sources:** [Garmin Forums](https://forums.garmin.com)

---

## 9. Tee Time Booking Platforms

### GolfNow
- **Website:** [golfnow.com](https://www.golfnow.com)
- **Type:** Tee time marketplace
- **Features:**
  - 9,000+ courses worldwide
  - 24/7 instant booking
  - Hot Deals with weather protection
  - GPS and rangefinder
  - Scorekeeping
  - GolfNow Rewards
  - GolfPass integration
- **Stats:** 3M+ golfers trust the app
- **Differentiator:** Largest marketplace, best deals
- **Sources:** [GolfNow](https://www.golfnow.com/mobile)

### Supreme Golf
- **Website:** (App Store)
- **Type:** Tee time aggregator
- **Features:**
  - Searches multiple sources
  - Price comparison
- **Sources:** [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/supreme-golf-book-tee-times/id909762897)

---

## 10. Amateur Golf Organizations

### USGA (United States Golf Association)
- **Website:** [usga.org](https://www.usga.org)
- **Type:** Governing body
- **Key Activities:**
  - Rules of Golf (with The R&A)
  - 14 national championships (10 amateur)
  - U.S. Open, U.S. Women's Open
  - GHIN handicap system
  - Curtis Cup, Walker Cup
- **Reach:** 25M+ golfers, 150+ golf associations
- **Sources:** [USGA](https://www.usga.org), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Golf_Association)

### Mediocre Golf Association
- See Social & Community section

---

## 11. Club Fitting Technology

### PING nFlight
- **Website:** [nflightweb.ping.com](https://nflightweb.ping.com)
- **Type:** Club fitting technology
- **Features:**
  - nFlight Web (static fitting)
  - nFlight Motion (3 shots = driver recommendation)
  - WebFit (online questionnaire)
  - Co-Pilot (fitter toolset)
  - 3D swing analysis
  - Integrates with launch monitors
- **Differentiator:** Industry-first virtual fitting software
- **Sources:** [PING nFlight](https://nflightweb.ping.com), [PING WebFit](https://webfit.ping.com)

### Cobra Connect
- **Website:** [cobragolf.com/pages/arccos](https://www.cobragolf.com/pages/arccos)
- **Type:** Pre-installed Arccos sensors
- **Features:**
  - Sensors embedded in Golf Pride grips
  - Full Arccos Caddie integration
  - Free sensors + 45-day trial with club purchase
  - Auto shot tracking
  - Strokes gained
- **Differentiator:** Only OEM with built-in shot tracking
- **Sources:** [Cobra Golf](https://www.cobragolf.com/pages/arccos), [Arccos Golf](https://www.arccosgolf.com/pages/cobra)

---

## Comparison: Sports Team Management (Non-Golf)

These platforms inspired Lazy Golfing's feature set:

### Project Hockey
- **Website:** [project.hockey](https://project.hockey)
- **Type:** Hockey league management
- **Features:**
  - Team rosters
  - Scheduling
  - Stats tracking
  - Standings
  - Communication

### TeamSnap
- **Website:** [teamsnap.com](https://www.teamsnap.com)
- **Type:** Multi-sport team management
- **Features:**
  - Scheduling
  - Availability tracking
  - Messaging
  - Payments
  - Stats
  - Photo sharing

---

## Market Gaps & Opportunities

Based on this research, Lazy Golfing can differentiate by:

1. **Unified Platform:** Most apps specialize (GPS OR betting OR leagues). Combine all.
2. **True League Management for Golf:** Golf Genius is enterprise-priced. Mid-market gap exists.
3. **Social + Competition:** MGA culture without chapter structure/geography limits.
4. **Free Tier:** Compete with TheGrint/18Birdies free features.
5. **Playing Partner Matching:** Deemples only works in Asia. US gap.
6. **No Hardware Lock-in:** Unlike Arccos/Garmin, work without sensors.
7. **Cart Golf Focus:** Unique positioning vs. serious golfer apps.

---

*This document should be updated as new competitors emerge or existing ones pivot.*

# Lazy Golfing Feature Roadmap

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Research Sources:** [Project Hockey](https://project.hockey), [TeamSnap](https://www.teamsnap.com)

---

## Vision

Create the most comprehensive golf platform that combines the best features of sports management platforms (like Project Hockey and TeamSnap) adapted specifically for golf. The platform should serve individual golfers, playing groups, leagues, and tournaments.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Lazy Golfing Feature Roadmap

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Research Sources:** [Project Hockey](https://project.hockey), [TeamSnap](https://www.teamsnap.com)

---

## Vision

Create the most comprehensive golf platform that combines the best features of sports management platforms (like Project Hockey and TeamSnap) adapted specifically for golf. The platform should serve individual golfers, playing groups, leagues, and tournaments.

---

## Feature Categories

### 1. Player Profiles & Stats

#### Personal Dashboard
- [ ] Handicap index display with trend graph
- [ ] Recent rounds summary
- [ ] Personal bests (lowest round, longest drive, most birdies)
- [ ] Season statistics overview
- [ ] Achievement badges/milestones
- [ ] Playing history timeline

#### Detailed Statistics
- [ ] Scoring average (overall, by course, by tee)
- [ ] GIR (Greens in Regulation) percentage
- [ ] FIR (Fairways in Regulation) percentage
- [ ] Putts per round / per hole
- [ ] Sand save percentage
- [ ] Up-and-down percentage
- [ ] Birdie/par/bogey/double distribution
- [ ] Scoring by hole par (par 3s, 4s, 5s)
- [ ] Front 9 vs Back 9 comparison
- [ ] Performance by time of day
- [ ] Performance by weather conditions

#### Player Cards
- [ ] Shareable player profile cards
- [ ] QR code for profile sharing
- [ ] Export stats to PDF

---

### 2. Round Tracking

#### Live Scoring
- [ ] Hole-by-hole scoring during round
- [ ] Real-time leaderboard for playing group
- [ ] GPS distance to pin (via integration)
- [ ] Quick-entry mode for fast scoring
- [ ] Voice input for hands-free entry

#### Post-Round Entry
- [ ] Full scorecard entry
- [ ] Bulk import from other apps
- [ ] Photo upload (scorecards, course photos)
- [ ] Notes per hole
- [ ] Playing partners tagging

#### Round Details
- [ ] Weather conditions (auto or manual)
- [ ] Tee selection (with course rating/slope)
- [ ] Equipment used (clubs, balls)
- [ ] Course conditions notes

---

### 3. Course Database

#### Course Information
- [ ] Course details (name, address, contact)
- [ ] Multiple tee options with ratings/slopes
- [ ] Hole-by-hole data (par, yardage, handicap)
- [ ] Course photos and aerial views
- [ ] Amenities (range, putting green, restaurant)
- [ ] Booking links

#### Course Reviews
- [ ] User ratings (condition, pace, value)
- [ ] Written reviews
- [ ] Photo uploads by users
- [ ] Tips for playing (local knowledge)

#### Course Search
- [ ] Search by location
- [ ] Filter by rating, price, difficulty
- [ ] "Courses near me" with map view
- [ ] Favorites/wishlist

---

### 4. League Management

*Inspired by Project Hockey's league management features*

#### League Setup
- [ ] Dedicated league page/section
- [ ] Custom league branding (logo, colors)
- [ ] League rules configuration
- [ ] Handicap allowance settings
- [ ] Division/flight setup
- [ ] Season archives

#### Scheduling
- [ ] Automated schedule generation
- [ ] Fair/balanced matchup algorithm
- [ ] Integration with course tee time booking
- [ ] Schedule sync to Google/iCal
- [ ] Rainout/reschedule management

#### Standings & Results
- [ ] Live standings updates
- [ ] Tiebreaker configurations
- [ ] Points system customization
- [ ] Week-by-week results
- [ ] Season-long statistics
- [ ] Historical season comparison

#### Team Management
- [ ] Team rosters
- [ ] Captain/manager delegation
- [ ] Sub/replacement player system
- [ ] Player availability tracking
- [ ] Team messaging

---

### 5. Tournament Play

#### Tournament Types
- [ ] Stroke play
- [ ] Match play (bracket management)
- [ ] Scramble/best ball
- [ ] Stableford
- [ ] Skins games
- [ ] Nassau
- [ ] Custom formats

#### Tournament Management
- [ ] Registration system
- [ ] Entry fee collection (payment integration)
- [ ] Handicap verification
- [ ] Flight/division assignments
- [ ] Tee time assignments
- [ ] Starting holes (shotgun starts)

#### Live Tournament Features
- [ ] Live leaderboard
- [ ] Hole-by-hole scoring
- [ ] Live scoring from course
- [ ] Spectator view (read-only leaderboard)
- [ ] Push notifications for key moments
- [ ] Social media sharing

#### Results & Awards
- [ ] Automated prize calculation
- [ ] Results export
- [ ] Certificate generation
- [ ] Photo gallery
- [ ] Winners archive

---

### 6. Playing Groups & Partners

#### Finding Partners
- [ ] Player search by location
- [ ] Skill level matching (handicap range)
- [ ] Availability calendar
- [ ] Playing preferences (pace, betting, etc.)
- [ ] "Looking for game" posts

#### Group Management
- [ ] Create private groups
- [ ] Group chat
- [ ] Group calendar
- [ ] Group statistics/leaderboards
- [ ] Group money games tracking

#### 2-somes, 4-somes, Groups
- [ ] Quick pairing suggestions
- [ ] Group round invitations
- [ ] RSVP tracking
- [ ] Automated reminders

---

### 7. Communication & Notifications

#### Messaging
- [ ] Direct messaging between players
- [ ] Group/team chat
- [ ] League announcements
- [ ] Tournament updates

#### Notifications
- [ ] Push notifications (mobile)
- [ ] Email notifications
- [ ] SMS alerts (optional)
- [ ] Notification preferences

#### Announcements
- [ ] League news/updates
- [ ] Course condition reports
- [ ] Weather alerts

---

### 8. Scheduling & Calendar

#### Personal Calendar
- [ ] Upcoming rounds
- [ ] League dates
- [ ] Tournament schedule
- [ ] Practice sessions
- [ ] Sync to Google/iCal/Outlook

#### Tee Time Integration
- [ ] Course booking links
- [ ] Group tee time coordination
- [ ] Availability polling
- [ ] Reminder notifications

---

### 9. Payments & Finance

#### Payment Collection
- [ ] League dues
- [ ] Tournament entry fees
- [ ] Group money games settlement
- [ ] Stripe/PayPal integration

#### Expense Tracking
- [ ] Green fee tracking
- [ ] Equipment purchases
- [ ] Annual golf expense summary

---

### 10. Mobile Experience

#### Mobile App Features
- [ ] Live scoring during rounds
- [ ] GPS distances
- [ ] Push notifications
- [ ] Quick stats lookup
- [ ] Camera integration for scorecards

#### Offline Support
- [ ] Offline round entry
- [ ] Sync when connected

---

### 11. Social Features

#### Activity Feed
- [ ] Recent rounds from connections
- [ ] League/tournament updates
- [ ] Achievements/milestones
- [ ] Comments and reactions

#### Sharing
- [ ] Share rounds to social media
- [ ] Share player cards
- [ ] Share tournament results

---

### 12. Integrations

#### Data Import
- [ ] GHIN handicap sync
- [ ] Import from other golf apps
- [ ] Bulk CSV import

#### External Services
- [ ] Weather API
- [ ] Course database APIs
- [ ] Mapping services (GPS)
- [ ] Calendar sync

---

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (Current)
- [x] User authentication (Google OAuth)
- [x] Basic player profiles
- [x] Round tracking (basic)
- [x] Course database (basic)
- [ ] Enhanced statistics

### Phase 2: Community
- [ ] Playing partner search
- [ ] Group management
- [ ] Messaging system
- [ ] Social feed

### Phase 3: Competition
- [ ] League management
- [ ] Tournament system
- [ ] Live scoring
- [ ] Leaderboards

### Phase 4: Advanced
- [ ] Mobile app (PWA or native)
- [ ] Payment integration
- [ ] GHIN integration
- [ ] Advanced analytics

---

## Competitive Differentiation

| Feature | Lazy Golfing | GHIN App | 18Birdies | GolfShot |
|---------|-------------|----------|-----------|----------|
| League Management | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Tournament Play | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Playing Partners | Yes (planned) | No | Yes | No |
| Team/Group Features | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Free Tier | Yes | Paid | Freemium | Freemium |
| Custom Branding | Yes (planned) | No | No | No |

---

## Priority Matrix

| Priority | Features | Impact | Effort |
|----------|----------|--------|--------|
| P0 | Basic round tracking, handicap | High | Low |
| P1 | Enhanced stats, groups | High | Medium |
| P2 | Leagues, tournaments | High | High |
| P3 | Live scoring, mobile app | Medium | High |
| P4 | Payments, integrations | Medium | Medium |

---

*This roadmap is a living document. Features will be prioritized based on user feedback and development capacity.*

content.text:
## Research Complete!

I've created a comprehensive competitive landscape document at `docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md` with **45+ platforms** organized into 11 categories:

**Key Sources Added:**
- **Shot Tracking:** Arccos, Shot Scope, Clippd, V1 Golf, Golf Pad
- **GPS/Rangefinder:** 18Birdies, Golfshot, GolfLogix, Bushnell, Garmin
- **Handicap/Scoring:** GHIN, TheGrint, SwingU, Hole19
- **League/Tournament:** Golf Genius, GolfStatus, DoJiggy, BlueGolf, Golf GameBook
- **Social/Community:** MGA Tour, GolfLync, Deemples, Golf Lounge, 19thole
- **Betting/Side Games:** Beezer Golf, Skins App, Unknown Golf, Golf Bettor
- **Launch Monitors:** TrackMan, Foresight GCQuad, Rapsodo
- **Club Fitting:** PING nFlight, Cobra Connect

---

## Header/Footer & Meta Tags Architecture

Regarding your question about the header/footer and meta tags:

### Current State

**Good news:** Next.js App Router already provides exactly what you want:

1. **Layout System:** The `layout.tsx` file wraps all pages. When you navigate between pages, **only the content changes** - the layout (header/footer) persists without re-rendering. This is built into Next.js's client-side navigation.

2. **Meta Tags:** OpenGraph and Twitter Cards are already implemented in `layout.tsx:34-57` as a single source of truth.

### What's Missing

The header/footer are **duplicated inside each page** rather than being in the shared layout. Here's what should be refactored:

```
Current (problematic):
├── layout.tsx          # Has metadata, but NO header/footer
├── page.tsx            # Has header + content + footer (duplicated)
├── about/page.tsx      # Has header + content + footer (duplicated)
└── login/page.tsx      # Has header + content + footer (duplicated)

Ideal (single source):
├── layout.tsx          # Has metadata + Header + Footer
├── page.tsx            # Just content
├── about/page.tsx      # Just content
└── login/page.tsx      # Just content (maybe different layout)
```

### Recommended Architecture

1. **Create shared components:**
   - `src/components/layout/Header.tsx`
   - `src/components/layout/Footer.tsx`

2. **Move to layout.tsx:**
   ```tsx
   // layout.tsx
   import { Header } from '@/components/layout/Header';
   import { Footer } from '@/components/layout/Footer';
   
   export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
     return (
       <html lang="en">
         <body>
           <Header />
           <main>{children}</main>
           <Footer />
         </body>
       </html>
     );
   }
   ```

3. **For pages that need different layouts (e.g., login):**
   - Create a separate layout: `src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx` with no header/footer
   - Move login page to: `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`

### Meta Tags (Already Good!)

Your current implementation is solid:
- **OpenGraph:** Lines 34-50 in layout.tsx
- **Twitter Cards:** Lines 51-57 in layout.tsx
- **Single source of truth:** `metadata` export in layout.tsx

To override per-page, just export metadata from the page:
```tsx
// src/app/about/page.tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: 'About - Lazy Golfing',
  openGraph: { title: 'About Lazy Golfing' }
};
```

### Schema.org (Not Yet Implemented)

For Schema.org structured data, you should add JSON-LD:

```tsx
// src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx
export function JsonLd({ data }: { data: object }) {
  return (
    <script
      type="application/ld+json"
      dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: JSON.stringify(data) }}
    />
  );
}

// In layout.tsx or pages:
<JsonLd data={{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebSite",
  "name": "Lazy Golfing",
  "url": "https://lazygolfing.com"
}} />
```

---

**Want me to refactor the header/footer into the layout now?** This would:
1. Create `Header.tsx` and `Footer.tsx` components
2. Move them to `layout.tsx`
3. Clean up `page.tsx` to only contain content
4. Add Schema.org JSON-LD component

'use client'; import Link from 'next/link'; import Image from 'next/image'; import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; export default function AboutPage() { return (
{/* Header */}
Lazy Golfing
{/* Main Content */}
{/* Background effects */}
{/* Logo */}
{/* Title */}

Welcome to Lazy Golfing

Lazy golf for lazy golfers.

{/* About Content */}

We believe golf should be fun, relaxed, and social. Whether you're tracking your handicap, competing in friendly leagues, or just keeping tabs on your rounds with friends — we've got you covered.

No walking required. No pretense. Just good golf with good people.

Cart golf only!

{/* Features */}
📊

Track Rounds

Log scores, stats, and track your progress over time.

🏆

Join Leagues

Compete with friends in organized league play.

👥

Connect

Find playing partners and grow your golf network.

{/* CTA */}
{/* Footer */}
© {new Date().getFullYear()} Lazy Golfing. All rights reserved.
); }

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout with ThemeProvider │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ ├── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature-specific components │ ├── lib/ │ │ └── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ │ ├── index.html # Old home page │ │ ├── profile.html # Old profile page │ │ ├── rounds.html # Old rounds page │ │ ├── js/ # Legacy JavaScript │ │ ├── css/ # Legacy CSS │ │ └── data/ # Generated JSON data │ └── [assets] # New site assets ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── page.tsx # / (home) ├── rounds/ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) ├── leagues/ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues ├── tournaments/ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments ├── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile └── login/ └── page.tsx # /login ``` ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

); } ``` ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).*

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout with ThemeProvider │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ ├── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature-specific components │ ├── lib/ │ │ └── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ │ ├── index.html # Old home page │ │ ├── profile.html # Old profile page │ │ ├── rounds.html # Old rounds page │ │ ├── js/ # Legacy JavaScript │ │ ├── css/ # Legacy CSS │ │ └── data/ # Generated JSON data │ └── [assets] # New site assets ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── page.tsx # / (home) ├── rounds/ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) ├── leagues/ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues ├── tournaments/ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments ├── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile └── login/ └── page.tsx # /login ``` ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

); } ``` ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).*
