Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ └── [assets] # New site assets (logo, og-image, etc.) ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── page.tsx # / (home) ├── rounds/ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) ├── leagues/ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues ├── tournaments/ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments ├── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile └── login/ └── page.tsx # /login ``` ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

); } ``` ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).*

## Routing

### App Router Structure
```
src/app/
├── layout.tsx            # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema)
├── page.tsx              # / (home) - content only
├── about/page.tsx        # /about
├── (auth)/               # Auth route group (minimal header)
│   ├── layout.tsx        # Auth-specific layout
│   └── login/page.tsx    # /login
├── (main)/               # Future: Pages with full nav
│   ├── rounds/
│   │   ├── page.tsx      # /rounds (list)
│   │   └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail)
│   ├── leagues/
│   │   └── page.tsx      # /leagues
│   ├── tournaments/
│   │   └── page.tsx      # /tournaments
│   └── profile/
│       └── page.tsx      # /profile
```

### Route Groups Explained

| Group | Layout | Use Case |
|-------|--------|----------|
| `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password |
| `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages |
| `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages |

### Link to Legacy Site
```tsx
// Link to old pages during migration:
<Link href="/alpha/login.html">Sign In (Legacy)</Link>
```

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ └── [assets] # New site assets (logo, og-image, etc.) ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/og-image.png` (1200x630) | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ └── [assets] # New site assets (logo, og-image, etc.) ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/og-image.png` (1200x630) | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

); } ``` ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return

} // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).*

content.text:
## All Done! Here's What Was Accomplished:

### 1. Golf Platform Research (45+ Competitors Analyzed)

Created `docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md` with comprehensive analysis:

| Category | Platforms Researched |
|----------|---------------------|
| Shot Tracking | Arccos, Shot Scope, Clippd, V1 Golf, Golf Pad |
| GPS/Rangefinder | 18Birdies, Golfshot, GolfLogix, Bushnell, Garmin |
| Handicap/Scoring | GHIN, TheGrint, SwingU, Hole19 |
| League/Tournament | Golf Genius, GolfStatus, DoJiggy, BlueGolf, Golf GameBook |
| Social/Community | MGA Tour, GolfLync, Deemples, Golf Lounge, 19thole |
| Betting/Side Games | Beezer Golf, Skins App, Unknown Golf, Golf Bettor |
| Launch Monitors | TrackMan, Foresight GCQuad, Rapsodo |

Updated `docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md` with quick reference table linking to all sources.

---

### 2. Architecture Refactor (Header/Footer/SEO)

**New File Structure:**
```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── layout/
│   │   ├── Header.tsx      # Single source for header
│   │   ├── Footer.tsx      # Single source for footer
│   │   └── index.ts
│   └── seo/
│       ├── JsonLd.tsx      # Schema.org structured data
│       └── index.ts
├── lib/
│   └── metadata.ts         # Centralized OpenGraph/Twitter/SEO config
└── app/
    ├── layout.tsx          # Header + Footer + Schema (persists)
    ├── page.tsx            # Content only (no header/footer)
    ├── about/page.tsx      # Content only + page metadata
    └── (auth)/
        ├── layout.tsx      # Minimal auth layout
        └── login/page.tsx  # Login content
```

**Key Benefits:**
1. **Header/Footer persist** across navigation (no re-render/flash)
2. **Single source of truth** - update once, changes everywhere
3. **OpenGraph, Twitter Cards, Schema.org** all centralized in `metadata.ts`
4. **Route groups** allow different layouts (auth vs main vs dashboard)

---

### 3. Documentation Updated

`lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` now includes:
- **Layout Architecture** section with rules and examples
- **SEO & Metadata** section with configuration guide
- **Route Groups** explanation
- Updated project structure diagram
- Checklist for creating new pages

---

### How to Add a New Page Going Forward:

```tsx
// src/app/courses/page.tsx
import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata';

export const metadata = createMetadata({
  title: 'Courses',
  description: 'Browse golf courses...',
  path: '/courses',
});

export default function CoursesPage() {
  return (
    <section className="w-full max-w-[1800px] mx-auto px-4 py-8">
      {/* Just content - Header/Footer come from layout.tsx */}
    </section>
  );
}
```

### To Update SEO Sitewide:

| What to Change | Where to Edit |
|----------------|---------------|
| Site name, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` |
| OG image | Replace `/public/og-image.png` |
| Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` |
| Nav links | `src/components/layout/Header.tsx` |
| Footer links | `src/components/layout/Footer.tsx` |

    95→        width: siteConfig.ogImageWidth,
    96→        height: siteConfig.ogImageHeight,
    97→        alt: siteConfig.ogImageAlt,
    98→      },
    99→    ],
   100→  },

   103→    title: `${siteConfig.name} - Track Your Golf Game`,

   105→    images: [siteConfig.ogImage],

   109→
   110→// =============================================================================
   111→// Viewport Configuration
   112→// =============================================================================
   113→
   114→export const viewportConfig = {
   115→  width: 'device-width',
   116→  initialScale: 1,
   117→  themeColor: [
   118→    { media: '(prefers-color-scheme: light)', color: '#ffffff' },
   119→    { media: '(prefers-color-scheme: dark)', color: '#0a0a0a' },
   120→  ],
   121→} as const;
   122→
   123→// =============================================================================
   124→// Metadata Factory Functions
   125→// =============================================================================
   126→
   127→/**
   128→ * Create page-specific metadata that merges with base metadata
   129→ *
   130→ * @example
   131→ * // In a page file:
   132→ * export const metadata = createMetadata({
   133→ *   title: 'Rounds',
   134→ *   description: 'Track and view your golf rounds',
   135→ *   path: '/rounds',
   136→ * });
   137→ */
   138→export function createMetadata(options: {
   139→  title: string;
   140→  description?: string;
   141→  path?: string;
   142→  image?: string;
   143→  noIndex?: boolean;
   144→}): Metadata {
   145→  const { title, description, path, image, noIndex } = options;
   146→  const url = path ? `${siteConfig.url}${path}` : siteConfig.url;
   147→  const ogImage = image || siteConfig.ogImage;
   148→
   149→  return {
   150→    title,
   151→    description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   152→    ...(noIndex && {
   153→      robots: {
   154→        index: false,
   155→        follow: false,
   156→      },
   157→    }),
   158→    openGraph: {
   159→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   160→      description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   161→      url,
   162→      images: [
   163→        {
   164→          url: ogImage,
   165→          width: siteConfig.ogImageWidth,
   166→          height: siteConfig.ogImageHeight,
   167→          alt: `${title} - ${siteConfig.name}`,
   168→        },
   169→      ],
   170→    },
   171→    twitter: {
   172→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   173→      description: description || siteConfig.shortDescription,
   174→      images: [ogImage],
   175→    },
   176→    alternates: {
   177→      canonical: url,
   178→    },
   179→  };
   180→}
   181→
   182→/**
   183→ * Create metadata for dynamic pages (e.g., course details, player profiles)
   184→ *
   185→ * @example
   186→ * // In a dynamic page [id]/page.tsx:
   187→ * export async function generateMetadata({ params }) {
   188→ *   const course = await getCourse(params.id);
   189→ *   return createDynamicMetadata({
   190→ *     title: course.name,
   191→ *     description: `Play ${course.name} - Par ${course.par}`,
   192→ *     path: `/courses/${params.id}`,
   193→ *   });
   194→ * }
   195→ */
   196→export function createDynamicMetadata(options: {
   197→  title: string;
   198→  description: string;
   199→  path: string;
   200→  image?: string;
   201→  type?: 'article' | 'profile' | 'website';
   202→  publishedTime?: string;
   203→  modifiedTime?: string;
   204→}): Metadata {
   205→  const {
   206→    title,
   207→    description,
   208→    path,
   209→    image,
   210→    type = 'website',
   211→    publishedTime,
   212→    modifiedTime,
   213→  } = options;
   214→
   215→  const url = `${siteConfig.url}${path}`;
   216→  const ogImage = image || siteConfig.ogImage;
   217→
   218→  return {
   219→    title,
   220→    description,
   221→    openGraph: {
   222→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   223→      description,
   224→      url,
   225→      type,
   226→      ...(publishedTime && { publishedTime }),
   227→      ...(modifiedTime && { modifiedTime }),
   228→      images: [
   229→        {
   230→          url: ogImage,
   231→          width: siteConfig.ogImageWidth,
   232→          height: siteConfig.ogImageHeight,
   233→          alt: title,
   234→        },
   235→      ],
   236→    },
   237→    twitter: {
   238→      title: `${title} | ${siteConfig.name}`,
   239→      description,
   240→      images: [ogImage],
   241→    },
   242→    alternates: {
   243→      canonical: url,
   244→    },
   245→  };
   246→}

public/

├── favicons/             # Other icon files
│   ├── favicon-16x16.png
│   ├── favicon-32x32.png

├── patterns/             # Background patterns
│   └── grid.svg
└── alpha/                # Legacy site
```

- Put everything else in organized folders

content.thinking:
The user asked about og-image dimensions - they made it 1024x1024 square and wondered if that's optimal. I need to answer this question and then continue with the public folder organization task.

Let me answer the og-image question first:

**OpenGraph Image Dimensions:**
- The standard/optimal size for OpenGraph images is **1200x630 pixels** (horizontal rectangle, 1.91:1 aspect ratio)
- This is the recommended size by Facebook, LinkedIn, and most platforms
- Twitter Cards prefer **1200x600** (2:1 ratio) for large summary cards, but 1200x630 works fine
- A 1024x1024 square will work but will be cropped on most platforms, which means important content on the edges could be cut off

I should recommend they update to 1200x630 for optimal display.

Then I need to continue with:
1. Mark the metadata task as complete (already done the code updates)
2. Audit the public folder structure
3. Create organized folder structure (favicons, images, etc.)
4. Update all references to new asset paths
5. Add file organization rules to CLAUDE.md (frontend)
6. Add workspace-wide organization rules to root CLAUDE.md

Let me first see what's currently in the public folder so I can organize it properly.

content.text:
For OG images, **1200x630 pixels (horizontal rectangle, 1.91:1 aspect ratio)** is the standard across platforms. A 1024x1024 square will display but gets cropped differently on each platform:

| Platform | Expected Ratio | What happens to 1024x1024 |
|----------|---------------|--------------------------|
| Facebook/LinkedIn | 1.91:1 (1200x630) | Cropped top/bottom |
| Twitter | 2:1 (1200x600) | Cropped top/bottom |
| Discord | 1.91:1 | Cropped top/bottom |
| iMessage/SMS | Varies | May crop edges |

**Recommendation:** Create at **1200x630** with important content centered in a safe zone (roughly 800x400 center area) to handle slight variations in cropping.

Now let me continue organizing the public folder. First, let me see what's currently there:

---

## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns)

### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer

**RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages.

```tsx
// src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern
import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body>
        <Header />           {/* Persists across navigation */}
        <main>{children}</main>
        <Footer />           {/* Persists across navigation */}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

**Why this matters:**

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ └── [assets] # New site assets (logo, og-image, etc.) ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/og-image.png` (1200x630) | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules)

**RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders.

### Folder Structure

```
public/
├── favicons/               # All favicon-related files
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   ├── favicon-16x16.png
│   ├── favicon-32x32.png
│   ├── apple-touch-icon.png
│   ├── android-chrome-192x192.png
│   └── android-chrome-512x512.png
├── images/                 # Photos, logos, OG images
│   ├── logo.png
│   └── og-image.png        # 1200x630 for social sharing
├── patterns/               # SVG patterns and textures
│   └── grid.svg
├── alpha/                  # Legacy site (don't modify)
└── site.webmanifest        # PWA manifest (references /favicons/)
```

### Asset Path Rules

| Asset Type | Folder | Example Path |
|-----------|--------|--------------|
| Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` |
| Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` |
| OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.png` |
| Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` |
| SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` |

### Social Sharing Image Specs

| Platform | Optimal Size | Notes |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio |
| Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) |
| Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations |

### Never Do This

```tsx
// ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/
src="/logo.png"
src="/og-image.png"
src="/grid.svg"

// ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders
src="/images/logo.png"
src="/images/og-image.png"
src="/patterns/grid.svg"
```

---

## Miami Vice Theming System

| Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file |

---

## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules)

**RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders.

### Folder Structure

```
public/
├── favicons/               # All favicon-related files
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   ├── favicon-16x16.png
│   ├── favicon-32x32.png
│   ├── apple-touch-icon.png
│   ├── android-chrome-192x192.png
│   └── android-chrome-512x512.png
├── images/                 # Photos, logos, OG images
│   ├── logo.png
│   └── og-image.png        # 1200x630 for social sharing
├── patterns/               # SVG patterns and textures
│   └── grid.svg
├── alpha/                  # Legacy site (don't modify)
└── site.webmanifest        # PWA manifest (references /favicons/)
```

### Asset Path Rules

| Asset Type | Folder | Example Path |
|-----------|--------|--------------|
| Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` |
| Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` |
| OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.png` |
| Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` |
| SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` |

### Social Sharing Image Specs

| Platform | Optimal Size | Notes |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio |
| Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) |
| Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations |

### Never Do This

```tsx
// ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/
src="/logo.png"
src="/og-image.png"
src="/grid.svg"

// ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders
src="/images/logo.png"
src="/images/og-image.png"
src="/patterns/grid.svg"
```

---

## Miami Vice Theming System

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/og-image.png` (1200x630) | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

---

## Brand Guidelines

### Voice
- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
- Celebrate small wins and funny losses

### Miami Vice Color Palette

| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

See [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.
